Class Parameter<T>

java.lang.Object
io.github.isoyigido.basic.gui.core.loader.Parameter<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the object type of the value contained in this parameter
Direct Known Subclasses:
AnchorParameter, ColorParameter, ImageResourceParameter, NumberParameter, StringParameter
public abstract class Parameter<T> extends Object

Represents a parameter in a GUI file. The generic value of the parameter should be parsable from a String representation. The parsing logic must be provided by the subclasses of this class by overriding the parse(String) method.

The parseAndSet(String) should be used to set the value of a parameter from a String representation in a GUI file. Additionally, a value of generic type may be set directly with the set(Object) method. To check whether a parameter has a set value, isPresent() and isEmpty() methods are provided. The get() method throws an UnsupportedOperationException if the parameter does not have a set value, and should only be used when it is known that the parameter has a set value. When it is not known whether a parameter has a set value, the getOptional() method may be used to obtain an Optional containing the value of the parameter, or an empty Optional if the parameter does not have a set value.

Example Usage:```java public class ColorParameter extends Parameter { @Override public Optional parse(String valueString) { // ... parse color hex (e.g., #52CBFF) or RGB values return Optional.of(new Color(r, g, b)); } }

See Also:
  • WidgetBuilder
  • GUILoader
API Note:
This abstract class is intended to make defining custom parameter types easier. By providing flexible parsing logic, the user may implement multiple ways to define a custom parameter. For example, a color may be parsed from both a hex value and integer RGB values. Further functionality may be added in the subclasses, like minimum and maximum values for an integer parameter. The parse(String) method implementation should log a warning and return an empty Optional if the input value is invalid in any way (e.g., out-of-bounds integer).

    value

    • value

      protected T value
      The value of this parameter (null indicates that no value is set)

    • Parameter

      public Parameter()

  • Method Details

    • get

      public T get() throws UnsupportedOperationException
      Returns the non-null value of this parameter.
      Returns:
      the non-null value of this parameter
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this parameter does not have a set value

    • getOptional

      public Optional<T> getOptional()
      Returns an Optional containing the non-null value of this parameter, or an empty Optional if this parameter does not have a set value.
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the non-null value of this parameter, or an empty Optional if this parameter does not have a set value
      See Also:
      • Optional

    • set

      public Parameter<T> set(T value)
      Sets the value of this parameter.
      Parameters:
      value - the new non-null value of this parameter
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input value is null
      API Note:
      This method sets the value of this parameter to the given literal non-null value. To remove the set value of this parameter, removeValue() must be used instead of parameter.set(null). Additionally, to set the value of this parameter based on a String representation (e.g., in a GUI file), parseAndSet(String) should be used to parse the String representation and set the value.

    • removeValue

      public Parameter<T> removeValue()
      Removes the set value of this parameter.
      Returns:
      this

    • isPresent

      public boolean isPresent()
      Returns whether this parameter has a set value.
      Returns:
      whether this parameter has a set value

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns whether this parameter does not have a set value.
      Returns:
      whether this parameter does not have a set value

    • parse

      public abstract Optional<T> parse(String valueString)

      Parses the given String representation and returns the corresponding generic parameter value.

      Override this method to provide parsing logic.

      Parameters:
      valueString - the String representation of the value
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the value of generic type, or an empty Optional if the given String representation is invalid in any way
      Implementation Requirements:
      Implementations of this method should log a warning and return an empty Optional if the input valueString is invalid in any way.

    • parseAndSet

      public Parameter<T> parseAndSet(String valueString)

      Parses the given String representation and sets the parsed value as the new value of this parameter.

      Special cases:

      • Does not set the value of this parameter if the given String representation is invalid
      Parameters:
      valueString - the String representation of the value
      Returns:
      this
      Implementation Note:
      This method uses parse(String) to parse the String representation, and if the returned Optional contains a value, sets it as the new value of this parameter using set(Object).
